name: Newburyport Five Cents Savings Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank serving Massachusetts and New Hampshire with deposit accounts, mortgages, and consumer loans.
description_long:
Newburyport Five Cents Savings Bank, operating as Newburyport Bank, is a traditional community bank headquartered in Newburyport, Massachusetts. The institution has established itself as a regional financial services provider with a presence across Massachusetts and New Hampshire. The bank emphasizes FDIC insurance protection and regulatory backing, positioning itself as a safe depository for consumer funds.

The bank offers a range of standard banking products including deposit accounts (checking and savings), Certificates of Deposit (CDs), personal consumer loans, and residential mortgages. Their current promotional offerings include 5-month CDs at 4.00% APY and 12-month CDs at 3.75% APY. The bank provides both personal and business online banking platforms, along with account opening capabilities through their website. Deposit rates and mortgage rates are publicly displayed, allowing consumers to compare offerings.

Newburyport Bank distinguishes itself through its community-focused approach and dual-state service area (MA and NH). The institution maintains traditional banking channels while modernizing its digital infrastructure, as evidenced by their announced website redesign. They offer separate business banking solutions through their Digital One Business Online Banking platform, indicating segment-specific service development.

As a traditional community bank, Newburyport Bank serves consumers seeking FDIC-insured deposit products and relationship-based lending. However, the limited information available on their website suggests they may have less aggressive rate competition compared to online-only banks, and their product offering appears more conservative than larger national institutions. The upcoming website redesign suggests recognition of digital experience gaps that may currently exist. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificate of Deposit (CD) products with variable terms and rates
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by federal government guarantee, protecting accounts up to insurance limits
Competitive CD rates advertised (4.00% APY for 5-month CDs) with full FDIC and DIF protection
Dual-state regional presence serving both Massachusetts and New Hampshire markets
Separate business banking platform (Digital One) available for commercial customers
Online account opening capability for consumer accessibility
Traditional relationship banking model with local community presence
Transparent rate disclosure for mortgages, consumer loans, and deposit products
cons: Limited product transparency on website; specific details about consumer loan terms, rates, and eligibility criteria not disclosed
No information about checking account features, minimum balance requirements, or fee structures
Online banking interface appears dated, with planned redesign suggesting current digital experience may lag competitors
No mention of investment services, financial advisory, or wealth management offerings
Minimal information about customer service availability, branch locations, or hours of operation
best_for: Massachusetts and New Hampshire residents seeking FDIC-insured savings and deposit accounts
Homebuyers in the MA/NH region shopping for mortgage financing with local lender relationships
Small business owners needing business checking and commercial lending relationships
diagnosis: Newburyport Bank is best suited for consumers in Massachusetts and New Hampshire who prioritize FDIC insurance protection and prefer relationship banking with a regional institution over online-only alternatives. The primary caveat is that limited public information about fee structures, specific product terms, and competitive rate positioning makes detailed comparison difficult; prospective customers should contact the bank directly for comprehensive product details before account opening.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
